rag.description: The SendKeys action types text into target UI elements with support for typing delay, clearing options, and keyboard modifier combinations. It targets elements via XPath or CSS locators and handles element-not-found scenarios by logging exceptions.

qa.question: What does the SendKeys action do?
qa.answer: SendKeys types text into a target UI element, simulating real keyboard input with configurable delay, clearing, and modifier key support.

qa.question: What parameters does SendKeys support?
qa.answer: Keys (text), Delay (typing speed), NativeClear (backspace clear), Clear (standard clear), and Modifier (Ctrl, Alt, Shift, Meta).

qa.question: How does element targeting work?
qa.answer: Elements are targeted using onElement with a locator strategy. Default locator is XPath. Regular expression patterns can filter element matching.

qa.question: When should NativeClear vs Clear be used?
qa.answer: NativeClear uses backspace simulation for custom controls. Clear uses standard WebDriver clear for simple inputs.

qa.question: How are modifier keys specified?
qa.answer: Modifier accepts a single string or JSON array: Control, Alt, Shift, or Meta. Keys are held during text input.

qa.question: What happens if the target element is not found?
qa.answer: A NoSuchElementException is logged and an empty response is returned. The workflow continues.

qa.question: Can SendKeys handle keyboard combinations?
qa.answer: Yes. SendModifiedKeys holds modifiers like Ctrl+A or Ctrl+C by specifying the modifier and key together.

description: ### Purpose
The SendKeys action types text into a target UI element such as input fields or text areas.
It provides flexible control over typing speed, content clearing, and keyboard modifier combinations.
This action is essential for automating form filling, search box input, and any text entry workflow.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature              | Description                                                                |
| Text Input           | Sends keystrokes to the element with configurable delay between characters |
| Clear Before Typing  | Supports native backspace clear and standard WebDriver clear               |
| Modifier Keys        | Press Ctrl, Alt, Shift, or Meta while sending text for shortcuts           |
| Flexible Text Source | Accepts text from the Keys parameter or rule argument                      |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case          | Description                                 |
| Form Field Entry  | Automatically fill text inputs in web forms |
| Search Box Input  | Type search queries into search fields      |
| Text Area Content | Enter multi-line text into text areas       |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case           | Description                                     |
| Input Validation   | Test form fields with various text inputs       |
| Keyboard Shortcuts | Test Ctrl+A, Ctrl+C type combinations           |
| Type Speed Testing | Verify typing behavior with configurable delays |

annotations.expected_result: The text 'Hello World' is typed into the input field.
annotations.notes: The action uses the Keys parameter from the argument macro. When Keys is empty, the argument itself is used as fallback text.

description: ### Send text to an input field
The rule targets an input element using XPath and sends the text 'Hello World' to it.
The element is located using the default Xpath locator strategy.

annotations.expected_result: The input field is cleared first, then 'New Text' is entered.
annotations.notes: NativeClear uses backspace simulation. Standard Clear uses WebDriver Clear method. Only one clear method should be used.

description: ### Clear and type new content
The rule clears the target element using native backspace simulation before sending the new text.
This is useful when replacing existing content in an input field.

annotations.expected_result: Ctrl+A is pressed to select all text in the element.
annotations.notes: Modifier keys are held down while the text is sent. Multiple modifiers can be provided as a JSON array.

description: ### Send keys with a modifier
The rule holds the Ctrl modifier while sending 'a' to trigger a Select All keyboard shortcut.
The modifier is provided as a JSON array containing Control.

edge_cases: One modifier key is not supported by the active driver
annotations.expected_result: The Control and Alt modifiers are held while the key 'a' is sent to the target element.
annotations.notes: Modifier accepts repeated parameter values that are converted into an array at runtime. The action holds all provided modifiers while sending the key value to the element.

description: ### Send a key with multiple modifiers
The rule holds the Control and Alt modifiers while sending the key `a` to the target element.
The repeated `--Modifier` parameters are converted into an array at runtime and applied together during the key send.

annotations.expected_result: The text is typed character by character with a 100ms delay between each keystroke.
annotations.notes: Delay accepts TimeSpan format like 00:00:00.100 for 100ms between characters. Default is zero delay for instant typing.

description: ### Type text with character delay
The rule sends text to an element with a 100ms delay between each keystroke.
This simulates human typing speed for applications that require realistic input timing.
Clear triggers a standard WebDriver clear operation before sending keys.
It removes existing content from input fields when present.
Use NativeClear for custom controls that do not respond to standard clear.
parameters.name: Clear

description: Delay sets the time interval between each keystroke.
Accepts TimeSpan format like 00:00:00.100 for 100ms delay between characters.
Set to 00:00:00 for instant typing without any delay.
parameters.name: Delay

description: Keys specifies the text string to send to the element.
This is the primary way to provide input text via parameter macro syntax.
When empty, the rule argument is used as the text to send.
parameters.name: Keys

description: Modifier specifies one or more keyboard modifier keys to hold while the key value is sent to the target element.
It accepts repeated parameter values that are converted into an array at runtime, such as `--Modifier:Control --Modifier:Alt`.
Use Modifier for keyboard combinations like Control+A, Shift+Tab, or multi-modifier shortcuts.
parameters.name: Modifier

description: NativeClear triggers a native backspace-based clear before sending keys.
It provides better compatibility with custom input controls.
Use this instead of Clear for elements that do not respond to standard clearing.
parameters.name: NativeClear

description: argument provides the keystroke text as a fallback when the Keys parameter is not set.
It allows passing text directly to the element without using a named parameter.
When both argument and Keys parameter are present, Keys takes precedence.
properties.name: argument

description: locator determines the strategy for finding the target element.
It defaults to Xpath for element location.
Supported locators include Xpath, CssSelector, Id, LinkText, and PartialLinkText.
properties.name: locator

description: onElement specifies the target element for sending keystrokes.
It is required for identifying which element receives the keyboard input.
The value format depends on the selected locator strategy.
properties.name: onElement

description: regularExpression filters element matching using pattern matching on element attributes.
It defaults to '(?si).*' to match all elements.
Use this to narrow down element selection when multiple elements match the locator.
properties.name: regularExpression

summary: The SendKeys action sends keystrokes to a target element in the UI.
It supports typing text with configurable delay between keystrokes.
The action can clear element content before typing using native or standard clear methods.
Modifier keys like Ctrl, Alt, and Shift can be held during text input.
